title: Experts on Experts: Why Compliance is becoming Continuous
description: This week on Experts on Experts, I’m joined by Sergio Alonso – Rapid7’s Director of Trust, Risk, and Compliance – to talk about how compliance is changing and why many security teams are rethinking the way they approach readiness, reporting, and operational risk.    One of the biggest themes in the conversation is that compliance is no longer something organizations can treat as a point-in-time exercise. Frameworks like NIS2 and DORA are increasing expectations around resilience and accountabili

Integrated directly into the Qumulo Data Platform, NeuralProtect inspects every file at the precise point-of-write using a series of AI-driven analysis models to detect both known and zero-day threats, instantly isolating malicious activity and enabling rapid recovery. description: Cruise giant Carnival has suffered yet another data breach, with ShinyHunters claiming to have stolen personal data affecting nearly 6 million people.

title: Qevlar’s new AI agents correlate CVEs, incident data, and active exploitation signals
description: Qevlar has announced a new set of AI agents designed to bridge the disconnect between Security Operations Centers (SOCs) and vulnerability management teams. The new capabilities help security teams correlate CVEs with live incident data for real-time risk prioritization, automatically identify asset owners to speed remediation, and autonomously hunt for active CVE exploitation. General availability is scheduled for Fall 2026. Finding and exploiting zero-day vulnerabilities has never been faster

title: Authenticated RCE via Argument Injection in Gogs (NOT FIXED)
description: Overview    Rapid7 Labs    discovered a critical argument injection (   CWE-88   ) vulnerability in    Gogs   , a popular open-source self-hosted Git service. Rapid7 Labs scores this vulnerability as    CVSSv4 9.4    (Critical). The vulnerability allows any authenticated user to achieve remote code execution (RCE) on the server by creating a pull request with a malicious branch name that injects the    --exec    flag into    git rebase    during the "Rebase before merging" merge operation. title: Hackers Abuse Shared CDN Edge IPs to Bypass Protective DNS Filtering
description:
Hackers are increasingly abusing shared Content Delivery Network (CDN) infrastructure to bypass protective DNS filtering, according to new research from ADAMnetworks, which has identified a stealthy technique allowing malicious traffic to hide behind trusted domains. The method, dubbed “Underminr,” exploits gaps in how security systems validate DNS requests, TLS connections, and CDN edge routing, enabling […]

description:

   Published :  May 28, 2026, 10:16 a.m. | 1 hour, 42 minutes ago

This issue was fixed in version 1.00B16CP.

title: CVE-2026-46241 - spi: mpc52xx: fix use-after-free on registration failure
description:
CVE ID : CVE-2026-46241
   
   Published :  May 28, 2026, 10:16 a.m. | 1 hour, 42 minutes ago
   
   Description : In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

spi: mpc52xx: fix use-after-free on registration failure

Make sure to disable and free the interrupts in case controller
registration fails to avoid a potential use-after-free and resource
leak.

This issue was flagged by Sashiko when reviewing a controller
deregistration fix.
   
   Severity:  0.0 | NA

internal buffers after FW releases") introduced a regression where
session_release_buf() may free the buffer. The caller,

title: CVE-2026-46239 - media: i2c: ov5647: Fix runtime PM refcount leak in s_ctrl
description:
CVE ID : CVE-2026-46239
   
   Published :  May 28, 2026, 10:16 a.m. | 1 hour, 42 minutes ago
   
   Description : In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

media: i2c: ov5647: Fix runtime PM refcount leak in s_ctrl

Three control cases (AUTOGAIN, EXPOSURE_AUTO, ANALOGUE_GAIN) directly
return without calling pm_runtime_put(), causing runtime PM reference
count leaks.

Change these cases from 'return' to 'ret = ... break' pattern to ensure
pm_runtime_put() is always cal

description:
CVE ID : CVE-2026-46238
   
   Published :  May 28, 2026, 10:16 a.m. | 1 hour, 42 minutes ago
   
   Description : In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

batman-adv: stop caching unowned originator pointers in BAT IV

BAT IV keeps the last-hop neighbor address in each neigh_node, but some
paths also cache an originator pointer derived from a temporary lookup.
That pointer is not owned by the neigh_node and may no longer refer to a
live originator entry after purge h

title: CVE-2026-46236 - media: rc: xbox_remote: heed DMA restrictions
description:
CVE ID : CVE-2026-46236
   
   Published :  May 28, 2026, 10:16 a.m. | 1 hour, 42 minutes ago
   
   Description : In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

media: rc: xbox_remote: heed DMA restrictions

The buffer for IO must not be part of the device structure
because that violates the DMA coherency rules.

title: CVE-2026-46235 - media: saa7164: add ioremap return checks and cleanups
description:
CVE ID : CVE-2026-46235
   
   Published :  May 28, 2026, 10:16 a.m. | 1 hour, 42 minutes ago
   
   Description : In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

media: saa7164: add ioremap return checks and cleanups

Add checks for ioremap return values in saa7164_dev_setup(). If
ioremap for BAR0 or BAR2 fails, release the already allocated PCI
memory regions, remove the device from the global list, decrement
the device count, and return -ENODEV.

This prevents potential n

title: A single typo could derail your World Cup plans
description: Cybercriminals are spoofing Fédération Internationale de Football Association (FIFA) websites ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, the FBI warns. The attackers are registering lookalike domains with small spelling changes or different domain endings to impersonate FIFA websites and services. The tactic, known as typosquatting, relies on users making small typing mistakes when entering website addresses. title: CVE-2025-48977: Apache Ignite: Rest Http default Arbitrary file read vulnerability
description:
Posted by zstan on May 28 Severity: Important 
 
Vendor: The Apache Software Foundation 
 
Versions Affected: Apache Ignite from 2.0.0 through 2.17.0. 
 
Impact: 
The attacker may be able to create or overwrite critical files that are   
used to execute code, such as programs or libraries. 
 
Description: 
Apache Ignite previously validated paths with a simple check like: 
src.path().startsWith(ctx.config().getIgniteHome()) 
This was unsafe because attackers could bypass it using path...
